title: Pale Nutty Irish Kombu Fried Rice
description: A light and crumbly Irish dish featuring kombu, with a nutty flavor, this fried rice is a unique and delicious meal option, kosher-friendly and made with khao khuah rice.
instructions: Cook the khao khuah rice according to package instructions and let it cool.
Rehydrate the kombu seaweed by soaking it in water for 30 minutes, then chop it into small pieces.
Heat the unsalted butter in a large skillet over medium heat.
Add the chopped onion and cook until it is translucent.
Add the minced garlic and cook for 1 minute.
Add the cooked rice, rehydrated kombu, sesame oil, salt, and black pepper to the skillet, stirring constantly.
Cook for 5 minutes, breaking up any clumps with a spatula.
Stir in the chopped scallions and cook for an additional minute.
